subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Mention the types of data. Write all built-in data types in C.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: What do you mean by data structure and mention its types?

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: What do you mean by an algorithm? Write the criteria and characteristics of an algorithm.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Demonstrate the efficiency of an algorithm.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Draw an AVL tree on following inputs, assume that tree is initially empty: 485, 575, 655, 745, 830, 910, 100, 110, 520, 130, 340, 450, 365, 525, 204, 155, 130, 35.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Compare and Contrast the Spanning tree and Minimum Spanning Tree.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Design an efficient algorithm for finding the longest directed path from a vertex s to a vertex of an acyclic weighted digraph G. Specify the graph representation used and any auxiliary data structures used. Analyze the time complexity of your algorithm.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Reconstruct the proof of correctness of the Bellman-Ford Algorithm. How Bellman-Ford algorithm able to work with negative weight?

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Determine the addressing formula to find the location of (i,j)th element of an m×n matrix stored in column-major order. Consider the linear arrays AAA[5:50], BBB[-5:10] and CCC[1:8]. i) Find the number of elements in each array. ii) Suppose base(AAA)=300 and w=4 words per memory cell for AAA. Find the address of AAA[15], AAA[35], and AAA[55].

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: What is a singly linked list? Write its advantage and application and explain its node structure. Write the algorithm and program in C to create and traverse a singly linked list.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: The problem of finding a subset T of the edges of a connected graph G such that all nodes remain connected when only the edges of T are used, and the sum of weights of edges in T is as small as possible, still makes sense even if G has edges with negative weights. Adapt either Kruskal's or Prim's algorithm to work on a graph that may include negative weights.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain heap and radix sort with the algorithm.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: How to measure the complexity of an algorithm, also discuss various types of notation for this purpose?

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: How doubly linked list is better than a linked list? Justify with examples.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Draw the directed graph that corresponds to the given adjacency matrix.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explore all types of operations that can be performed on a linked list.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: What is Abstract data type? Explain with the help of example.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Differentiate array and linked list.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: What is the application of linked list. And also write the algorithm how to add two polynomials using linked list.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Write a program in C to insert a node at any specified position in doubly linked list.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: What is the difference between data and information? Give one example of each.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: How to represent 2-D array in memory? Explain with the help of example.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Write an algorithm to convert infix expression into postfix form using Stack. Also evaluate the given postfix form using stack.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Write a program in 'C' to implementation of QUEUE.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: What are the differences between: i) Height and depth ii) Order and degree.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Show that the maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h is 2^(h+1)-1.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Construct the steps to configure a B-tree of order 5 for the given data.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain Dijkstra Algorithm with the help of example.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Write a Heap sort algorithm. Use Heap sort algorithm to sort the following elements: D A T A S T R U C T U R E S.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Differentiate internal sorting and external sorting. Also enlists the name of two sorting techniques of each.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Binary search is more efficient than Linear search. Justify your answer.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Write short note on Hashing and Indexing.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Construct a Binary tree for the given traversals (Postorder and Inorder). Also, find the Preorder traversal of the Binary tree so formed.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: How a tree can be stored in the memory? Explain with an example. Explain the advantages and disadvantages of the array representation of a tree.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Construct an expression tree for the algebraic expression (3a-b)²(4c+2d)³.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain the Binary Search Tree with an example. Make a BST for the given sequence and perform deletions. Also write an algorithm to insert an element into a BST.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Define AVL Trees. Explain its rotation operations with example. Construct AVL Tree by inserting the given data.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Define Huffman tree and describe an algorithm to create a Huffman tree. Construct a Huffman Tree for data structures with its optimal code.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Explain in detail about the graph traversal techniques with suitable example.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Consider the given undirected graph. Find the adjacency list representation and find a minimum cost spanning tree by Kruskal's algorithm.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Write the algorithm to create a heap and sort the given elements using heap sort.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: How do you implement a circular queue in C using array? Write routines to implement operations for it. Differentiate between Linear Queue and Circular Queue.

Define doubly linked list. Describe an algorithm to insert an element at the beginning of the doubly linked list. Describe an algorithm to delete an element from the end of doubly linked list. Also explain the advantages and disadvantages of doubly linked list.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Given a 2D array A[-100:100, -5:50]. Find the address of element A[99, 49] considering the base address 10 and each element requires 4 bytes for storage. Follow row-major order.
